policies.name: Source code exfiltration to personal accounts
policies.description: An export request that combines repository content with a data-export intent — for example mailing the codebase to a personal address or syncing a private repo to a personal cloud drive — is interdicted outright. Once source leaves controlled infrastructure the trade-secret status of the crown-jewel asset cannot be restored.
regulatory_references: Common law duty of confidence — Coco v A N Clark (Engineers) Ltd [1969] RPC 41
Industry best practice — source code exfiltration is irreversible trade-secret destruction

policies.name: Repository access grant to external party
policies.description: Structured agent actions that add an outside collaborator or share a repository link grant standing access to technical IP, not a one-off disclosure. A human must confirm the contractor agreement and least-privilege scope before the grant executes.

policies.name: Proprietary algorithm description request
policies.description: Requests to explain how the ranking algorithm, pricing model internals or matching engine actually work are a classic soft-extraction vector: each answer alone looks harmless, together they reconstruct the secret. IP counsel approves what level of abstraction may be shared.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — algorithm internals are protectable only while kept confidential

policies.name: Departure-pattern bulk code collection
policies.description: Language typical of pre-departure hoarding — bulk-cloning everything, mirroring repos, taking a copy of the codebase before a last day — matches the dominant fact pattern in APAC trade-secret litigation. The insider-threat desk takes over to preserve evidence and stop the collection.
regulatory_references: Korea Unfair Competition Prevention and Trade Secret Protection Act art. 2 — Trade Secret Infringement
China Anti-Unfair Competition Law art. 9 — Trade Secret Infringement

policies.name: Pre-publication review of engineering content
policies.description: Engineering blog posts, conference talks and whitepapers routinely leak patentable subject matter or system internals without anyone intending to. A lightweight IP review gate on these structured publication tasks catches novelty-destroying detail before it goes public.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — pre-publication IP review prevents inadvertent trade-secret and patent-novelty loss

policies.name: Permissively licensed code reuse (flag)
policies.description: Incorporating MIT, Apache or BSD licensed components is routine engineering work and proceeds without a gate, but it is flagged so the OSS inventory stays current and attribution notices are not silently dropped.

policies.name: Trade-secret formula in outbound content
policies.description: Secret formulas, proprietary formulations and manufacturing recipes lose statutory protection the moment reasonable secrecy measures lapse, so the engine refuses to compose or transmit content containing them. There is no approval path because no recipient outside the secrecy perimeter is acceptable by default.
regulatory_references: Japan Unfair Competition Prevention Act art. 2 — Acts of Unfair Competition (Trade Secrets)
China Anti-Unfair Competition Law art. 9 — Trade Secret Infringement

policies.name: Unsolicited competitor confidential material
policies.description: When a competitor's confidential material arrives unsolicited — a leaked roadmap, a former employer's documents, their internal pricing sheet — the safe response is clean-room handling: quarantine it, do not read further, and let counsel manage return or destruction. Using it taints every downstream work product.
regulatory_references: Common law duty of confidence — third-party recipient liability for information received in confidence

policies.name: Use of misappropriated confidential material
policies.description: Any instruction to incorporate stolen trade secrets, leaked source code or other illegally obtained documents into our work is refused outright. Knowing use of misappropriated material is itself an infringing act under every APAC trade-secret regime, independent of who took it.
regulatory_references: China Anti-Unfair Competition Law art. 9 — Trade Secret Infringement
Korea Unfair Competition Prevention and Trade Secret Protection Act art. 2 — Trade Secret Infringement

policies.name: Manufacturing process detail release
policies.description: Suppliers and auditors legitimately need some process detail, but process specifications, equipment calibration settings, yield data and tooling drawings are exactly the know-how that walks out through the supply chain. Counsel confirms the confidentiality terms cover the specific items before release.
regulatory_references: Japan Unfair Competition Prevention Act art. 2 — Acts of Unfair Competition (Trade Secrets)

policies.name: Reverse-engineering assistance request
policies.description: Asking the agent to reverse engineer, decompile a binary, disassemble firmware or extract model weights sits in legally uneven territory across APAC — lawful in some markets for interoperability, contractually barred in most licence terms. Counsel decides per target and jurisdiction.

policies.name: Publicly documented technique discussion
policies.description: Explaining a published research paper, a textbook method, an open standard or a publicly documented API carries no confidentiality exposure — public information cannot be a trade secret — so the agent handles it without escalation.

policies.name: Inside information in outbound communication
policies.description: An agent action that would push M&A code-name material, a pending acquisition or other material non-public information to an external recipient or public channel before announcement is interdicted — this is the communication leg of insider-trading liability, not merely a confidentiality lapse. No customer configuration may relax it.
regulatory_references: Singapore Securities and Futures Act s.218 — Prohibited Conduct by Connected Persons
Hong Kong Securities and Futures Ordinance Part XIVA — Disclosure of Inside Information
Australia Corporations Act 2001 s.1043A — Insider Trading Prohibition

policies.name: Unreleased earnings material handling
policies.description: Quarterly results, profit guidance and unpublished financials are inside information until the exchange announcement is made, and selective pre-release is a listing-rule breach in every APAC market we operate in. The disclosure committee owns every touch of this material — statutory duty, never automatable.
regulatory_references: SGX Listing Rules Rule 703 — Disclosure of Material Information
ASX Listing Rule 3.1 — Continuous Disclosure
Hong Kong Securities and Futures Ordinance Part XIVA — Disclosure of Inside Information

policies.name: Share-price-sensitive incident in messages
policies.description: A message describing an incident as market sensitive, moving the share price or warranting a trading halt may itself create a continuous-disclosure obligation with an immediacy clock. The disclosure committee must assess materiality before any automated reply characterises or spreads the information.
regulatory_references: ASX Listing Rule 3.1 — Continuous Disclosure
SGX Listing Rules Rule 703 — Disclosure of Material Information

policies.name: Pre-announcement trading or tipping language
policies.description: Phrases proposing to buy shares before, sell before the news, trade before the announcement, or tip someone off describe procuring and tipping offences, not just policy breaches. Compliance surveillance takes the conversation over immediately and preserves the record.
regulatory_references: Singapore Securities and Futures Act s.219 — Prohibited Conduct by Other Persons
Australia Corporations Act 2001 s.1043A — Insider Trading Prohibition

policies.name: Deal data room access and content requests
policies.description: Requests touching a data room, due diligence materials or deal documents sit inside the M&A perimeter where every access is logged against the insider list. A human approver confirms the requester is wall-crossed before the agent grants access or summarises content.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong Securities and Futures Ordinance Part XIVA — Disclosure of Inside Information

policies.name: Insider list and wall-crossing administration
policies.description: Adding names to an insider list, executing a wall crossing or amending the restricted list changes who may lawfully hold inside information, so the change itself needs a named human owner. The agent may draft but not commit these records.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — insider/restricted list maintenance under information-barrier controls

policies.name: Already-announced information reuse
policies.description: Information that is already announced, contained in a public filing or an annual report has ceased to be inside information, so summarising or recirculating it is safe for full automation.

policies.name: Copyleft licence contamination check
policies.description: Pulling GPL or AGPL licensed code into a proprietary codebase can trigger an obligation to release our own source — a commercial outcome no engineer should commit to unilaterally. The OSS compliance desk approves the component, the linkage model and any isolation strategy.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — GPL/AGPL copyleft obligations can compel proprietary source disclosure

policies.name: Stock photo, image and font licence verification
policies.description: Stock photos, stock images and fonts are the most common source of copyright demand letters in APAC marketing operations because seat counts, media types and territories are licensed narrowly. A human verifies the specific licence covers the proposed use before assets ship.
regulatory_references: Singapore Copyright Act 2021 — Exclusive Rights of Copyright Owners
Australia Copyright Act 1968 s.36 — Infringement by Doing Acts Comprised in Copyright

policies.name: AI-generated content imitating protected marks or characters
policies.description: Generation requests invoking a famous cartoon character, a trademarked logo, a celebrity likeness or a lookalike mascot put the company on the wrong side of copyright, trademark and personality-rights claims simultaneously. IP counsel handles these directly because near-miss variants are still actionable.
regulatory_references: Japan Copyright Act — reproduction and adaptation rights in characters and artistic works

policies.name: Third-party code snippet provenance check
policies.description: A stack overflow snippet or code copied from github arrives with licence terms most contributors never check, and vendored code drifts out of its notice obligations. A reviewer confirms provenance and attribution before the commit lands in a shipping product.

policies.name: Comparative marketing using competitor marks
policies.description: A comparison chart, competitor benchmark or other comparative advertising names a rival's marks and invites trademark and misleading-conduct exposure if any claim is unsubstantiated. Counsel signs off the substantiation file before the material is published.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — comparative advertising requires substantiated claims and nominative trademark use

policies.name: NDA-covered partner material reuse across customers
policies.description: Material received under NDA from one partner cannot be repackaged for another relationship — purpose-limitation clauses survive even when the content looks generic. When a request references partner confidential or NDA-covered material in a new context, counsel maps the permitted-purpose clause before anything moves.
regulatory_references: Common law duty of confidence — purpose limitation on information disclosed under NDA

policies.name: Customer deliverable reuse for another client
policies.description: Asking to reuse the deliverable or repurpose the report from a previous engagement for another client raises a contract-by-contract ownership question: many APAC services agreements assign deliverable IP to the paying customer. An approver checks who owns the work product before any reuse.

policies.name: Partner access termination at contract end
policies.description: Contract-end offboarding tasks must revoke every repository, drive and portal grant the partner accumulated, and confidentiality clauses usually require certified return or destruction of our material. A human signs off the revocation checklist rather than trusting a single automated sweep.

policies.name: Vendor due-diligence disclosure response
policies.description: A due diligence questionnaire, security questionnaire or vendor assessment invites detailed disclosure of architecture, controls and sometimes customer information, and over-answering is the norm when automated. A human owner curates the response pack against what the diligence NDA actually permits.

policies.name: Standard NDA template issuance
policies.description: Issuing the pre-approved mutual NDA template from the legal library is deliberately self-service — it is the control that makes everything else safe, so the agent completes these structured requests without escalation.

policies.name: Product roadmap disclosure to outside parties
policies.description: Sharing the product roadmap, unreleased features or an upcoming release plan outside the company is only acceptable behind a signed confidentiality agreement, and sales teams routinely skip that step under deal pressure. An approver verifies NDA status before the roadmap deck leaves.

policies.name: Public disclosure before patent filing
policies.description: Describing an unfiled invention publicly destroys absolute novelty in Singapore, Japan, Korea, China and most APAC patent systems — grace periods are narrow and unreliable. When a message ties a patent application or invention disclosure to a not-yet-filed state, patent counsel takes over before a priority date is forfeited.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — public disclosure before filing destroys patent novelty in absolute-novelty jurisdictions

policies.name: Unfiled invention detail shared with vendors
policies.description: A prototype demonstration or technical brief that exposes invention details to a vendor before filing is a controlled disclosure only if a confidentiality agreement is in place first. The approver's single question — is the disclosure covered — protects both novelty and ownership of improvements.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — vendor disclosures of unfiled inventions require prior confidentiality undertakings

policies.name: Granted or published patent discussion
policies.description: Analysing a granted patent, a published patent application or an expired patent uses material the patent system has already made public, so freedom-to-operate style discussion proceeds without a gate.

policies.name: Embargoed press material handling
policies.description: Material marked under embargo or embargoed until a set time — including any press release draft — must not be summarised, forwarded or quoted by an automated agent, because a single early send breaks the embargo for every outlet at once. Corporate comms controls timing and distribution end to end.

policies.name: Prototype imagery on social channels
policies.description: A prototype photo, an unreleased device shot, an engineering sample image or a behind the scenes photo posted socially can leak design IP, void patent novelty and pre-announce a launch in one move. Brand protection clears the imagery before anything is scheduled for posting.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — pre-launch imagery is both trade-secret and patent-novelty exposure

policies.name: Reseller misuse of brand assets
policies.description: Reports of unauthorized logo use, a modified version of our logo, or a counterfeit listing in a reseller channel need an enforcement decision — cease-and-desist, marketplace takedown or partner remediation — that weighs the commercial relationship. Brand protection runs the playbook; the agent only gathers evidence.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — trademark enforcement via graduated takedown and partner remediation

policies.name: Brand asset use within published guidelines
policies.description: Employee and partner requests that explicitly work from the brand guidelines or the official logo pack are the sanctioned path for mark usage, so the agent fulfils them directly while logging usage for the brand team.
